preview.summary: Two AFC and CAF heavyweights meet in a Group J clash likely pivotal for second place behind Argentina. Jordan rely on pace and Al-Taamari's craft on the break, while Algeria's individual quality up front gives them the edge in a tight, physical contest.
preview.homeForm: Jordan are at their first World Cup after a 2023 Asian Cup final run, ranked around 63rd.
preview.awayForm: Algeria topped their CAF group to reach a fifth World Cup, with Amoura among qualifying's top African scorers, ranked around 28th.
preview.homeKey: Musa Al-Taamari, Rennes winger and Jordan's main attacking threat.
preview.awayKey: Mohamed Amoura, prolific Wolfsburg forward who fired Algeria to the finals.
storylines: A possible decider for second in Group J
Mahrez likely playing his final World Cup for Algeria
Jordan's debutants against the 2019 AFCON champions
preview.h2h: The teams have met once before, finishing level with no decisive history.
